I couldn't find any information about the plant "Brassica prolongi." However, there is a plant called "Brassica juncea" which is a type of mustard plant and might be a similar plant.

If we assume that "Brassica prolongi" is a similar plant, here is a paragraph about Brassica juncea:

Brassica juncea, also known as mustard greens or brown mustard, is a plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family, which includes cabbage, broccoli, and kale. The health benefits of Brassica juncea include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its high content of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The bioactive constituents of Brassica juncea include glucosinolates, isothiocyanates, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anticancer properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Brassica juncea include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
